quotes.text: WHOEVER... is pure in heart, who is ever holy in his life, and free from deceit.
IN whom there is no deceit, no arrogance, who has mixed with no (other) desires, who is free from selfishness, who is without desire, who has driven away anger, who has his self quenched.
PURIFY your thoughts, keep your tongue from evil, let your body be free from sin.
A MAN should avoid all ... sinful acts, as a merchant, with few companions and carrying much wealth, avoids a dangerous road.
ABSTAINING from all things that ought to be avoided.
WHATsoever is to be found is necessarily... accompanied by sorrow.
WHETHER a mother, or a father, or any other relative is guilty of a crime, ... you should not conceal it.
OVERCOME anger by love; overcome greed by liberality; overcome falsehood by truth.
HATRED does not cease by hatred at any time: hatred ceases by love, this is an old rule.
WITH a pure heart, full of love, I will also observe the moral precepts.
LET the (wise) man... live a life of love, and fill the four quarters of the world with thoughts of love.
quotes.source: Questions of King Milinda (Book 4, ch. 4, sec. 16)

quotes.text: THE love which a father or mother has for their child... is but a small portion of that love which (the Buddha) has for all beings.
quotes.source: Questions of King Milinda (Book 4, ch. 1, sec. 10)

quotes.text: HE (the Buddha) is the Father of all the world, and all sentient beings are his children.
LOVE all... as if they were your only son.
THE teacher of the world... like a mother's love for her child, looks upon all beings with equal eyes.
IF anyone should speak ill of you, you should not on that account cherish any suspicion or resentment of him.
IF you are spoken to crossly, ... let your heart be firm, and let no angry word escape your lips.
quotes.source: Questions of King Milinda (Book 4, ch. 4, sec. 26)

quotes.text: HE who is tolerant with the intolerant... and free from greed among the greedy—him I call indeed a (wise man).
TO those who do us harm, we will return love.
AS a mother at the risk of her life watches over her own child, her only child, so also let everyone cultivate a boundless (friendly) mind towards all beings.
ABOVE, below, and all around, unstinted love, unmarred by any sense of differing interest.
THE world is full of sorrow, but I will go and preach the doctrine of the heart to those who are suffering.
HE (the Buddha) has no other thought than that of doing good.
HE is a man who takes no account of his own life, and is ever ready to give himself for others.
TO work for the benefit of all the world is my duty.
LET a man... overcome his own selfishness by acts of kindness.
LET a man... share his possessions with others.
WE should give what we have to those who are in need.
LET a man... give to the poor, and let him give without being asked.
GIVE to him who asks, and even to him who does not ask.
